output.final_answer:
Diagnosis of Current Organization:
The drive is partially organized by top‑level folders (Documents, Downloads, Pictures, Projects). Documents contains only financial text files, which are well‑named and shallow, earning an "excellent" score. Projects shows a clear hierarchy with Session7/Session8 and code subfolders, rated "good". Downloads mixes receipts, notes, and miscellaneous files without subfolders, and Pictures holds text files named like images plus duplicate backups, leading to lower organization scores.

Classification Summary (based on filename and metadata):
- Documents/Form16_FY2025.txt → document (Finance/TaxDocuments/2025)
- Documents/HDFC_statement_Jan2026.txt → document (Finance/BankStatements/2026)
- Documents/PAN_card.txt → document (Finance/IDDocuments)
- Documents/salary_slip_april2026.txt → document (Finance/SalarySlips/2026)
- Downloads/amazon_order_4471.txt → receipt (Finance/Receipts/Orders)
- Downloads/electricity_bill_jan.txt → receipt (Finance/Receipts/Bills)
- Downloads/flipkart_2025_tv.txt → receipt (Finance/Receipts/Orders)
- Downloads/swiggy_invoice_mar.txt → receipt (Finance/Receipts/Orders)
- Downloads/old_notes_2023.txt → document (Documents/Notes) – needs review
- Downloads/untitled.txt → other (Misc) – needs review
- Pictures/IMG_2098.txt → other (Misc) – needs review
- Pictures/sunset.txt → other (Misc) – needs review
- Pictures/IMG_2098_copy.txt → other (Misc) – needs review
- Pictures/sunset_dup.txt → other (Misc) – needs review
- Projects/EAGv3/Session7/README.md → code (Projects/Documentation)
- Projects/EAGv3/Session7/code/flow.py → code (Projects/Code)
- Projects/EAGv3/Session8/README.md → code (Projects/Documentation)
- Projects/EAGv3/Session8/code/flow.py → code (Projects/Code)

Sensitive/Financial Files Flagged (never read content):
- Documents/Form16_FY2025.txt – tax form indicator
- Documents/HDFC_statement_Jan2026.txt – bank statement
- Documents/PAN_card.txt – government ID
- Documents/salary_slip_april2026.txt – payroll document
All four are marked as sensitive candidates with high confidence.

Duplicate Files and Reclaimable Space:
1. Hash dc60fc3044c8: Pictures\IMG_2098.txt and Pictures\backup\IMG_2098_copy.txt (each 10 bytes) → reclaim 10 bytes.
2. Hash 334953bab38c: Pictures\sunset.txt and Pictures\backup\sunset_dup.txt (each 10 bytes) → reclaim 10 bytes.
Total reclaimable space from duplicates: approximately 20 bytes.

Phase‑1 Recommendations:

Minimal Effort (~5 min):
- Delete duplicate copies: Pictures\backup\IMG_2098_copy.txt
- Delete duplicate copies: Pictures\backup\sunset_dup.txt
- Delete installer‑extract folder: Downloads\SetupTool_v3_extracted (4 files)
- Reclaimable: ≈20 bytes (duplicates) + size of installer files (likely <1 KB)
- Risk: very_low

Medium Effort (~15 min) – includes all minimal actions plus:
- Create subfolders in Downloads:
  • Downloads\Orders (move amazon_order_4471.txt, flipkart_2025_tv.txt)
  • Downloads\Bills\2026 (move electricity_bill_jan.txt, bills\swiggy_invoice_mar.txt)
  • Downloads\Archive (move old_notes_2023.txt, untitled.txt)
- Create Pictures\Originals and move IMG_2098.txt, sunset.txt there
- Delete empty Pictures\backup folder after moving originals
- Reclaimable: ≈20 bytes (duplicates) + any space freed by removing empty backup folder
- Risk: low
- Recommended: true

Best Effort (~30‑45 min) – includes all medium actions plus:
- Rename files for a uniform pattern (YYYYMMDD_Description.txt) across Downloads and Pictures
- In Downloads, further nest by year:
  • Downloads\2026\Orders (amazon_order_4471.txt, flipkart_2025_tv.txt)
  • Downloads\2026\Bills (electricity_bill_jan.txt, swiggy_invoice_mar.txt)
- Create a top‑level Images folder:
  • Images\Originals (IMG_2098.txt, sunset.txt)
  • Images\Backup (remove after confirming originals are safe)
- Apply the same naming convention inside Projects if desired (optional, no file moves)
- Document the new hierarchy in a short README.md at the drive root
- Reclaimable: ≈20 bytes (duplicates) + space from removed backup folder; no additional size gain
- Risk: medium

This phase‑1 report provides a clear diagnosis, classification of files, identification of sensitive/financial items, duplicate cleanup opportunities, and tiered action plans to improve organization with minimal risk.

prompt_sent:
You are the Formatter skill. You are the conventional TERMINAL node of
every DAG. Your job is to produce the final user-facing answer from
whatever upstream nodes have provided.

You make no tool calls. The user's original query appears under
USER_QUERY. Upstream results appear under INPUTS.

Procedure:
  1. Read USER_QUERY.
  2. Read INPUTS and decide which fields / findings answer the query.
  3. Write the user-facing answer in plain English. Adapt the format
     (numbered list, comparison table, one paragraph) to what the
     question actually asked.

Output schema (JSON, no prose, no markdown fences):

  {
    "final_answer": "<the answer the user sees>"
  }

Rules:
  - This is the LAST node. Do not add successors.
  - The answer must be answerable from INPUTS alone. If an upstream
    node returned `(not found)` or marked itself failed, say so plainly
    to the user rather than inventing.
  - Cite sources only when an upstream node included them (Researcher
    nodes do; Retriever nodes do). Do not invent URLs.

USER_QUERY: Analyse and organise this folder. Diagnose how well it is currently organised, classify each non-sensitive file batch (documents, photos, receipts) in parallel, flag any private/financial files from metadata only (never read their content), compute duplicate files and reclaimable space, and produce a phase-1 report with minimal/medium/best effort plans.
